decision_rationale: no_per_pass_fps_evidence: the GRX contract requires a per-pass FPS >= 0.95x baseline measured_local comparison before any default enablement (GRX_PLAN GRX.4 exit criteria / GRX_CONTRACT G-GRX-5 single_scene_fps_ratio_min=0.95), and no full baseline or per-pass benchmark comparison for taa_resolve exists yet

patch_0019_writeback_is_scaffold: the single full-resolution resolve result is dispatched into the real Godot taa/temp destination texture while the native Godot TAA resolve continuation still re-resolves every view AND maintains the resolve->temp->internal->history physical copy chain every frame as the backstop, so the candidate sequence cannot change (temporal LDR visual gate max_abs=0/mean_abs=0 at every captured frame) and the opt-in Rurix dispatch yields no measured net benefit yet

single_resolve_subset_and_one_frame_latency: math parity covers only the single full-resolution TAA resolve subset (math_parity_evidence.json status=pending_gpu_dispatch); the history double-buffer copy-back / prev_velocity bookkeeping, the one-frame-latency draw_graph true replacement, hardware-sampler sub-texel rounding, rgba16f/rg16f half storage quantization, and multiview are recorded gaps
does_not_imply: temporal-stability or visual-superiority claim (candidate is frame-for-frame bit-exact to the reference sequence; the writeback is a scaffold)
broader taa_resolve coverage (history maintenance chain, one-frame-latency true replacement, multiview) beyond the single full-resolution resolve subset
net-benefit Rurix dispatch (patch 0019 result writeback is a scaffold; the native Godot TAA resolve re-resolves and maintains history every frame)
